---
title: "The One Piece: Dataset"
author: "Jezel V., John E., Shelby T."
output: ioslides_presentation
date: "2026-04-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

# Project Overview: 

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="70%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("https://logos-world.net/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/One-Piece-Logo-1999.png")
```

- Exploring patterns in the One Piece world
- Focusing on bounties, characters, Devil Fruits, Islands, and episodes.

# Introduction 
- One piece is a long running anime (1999) and manga series 
- The world full of pirates, crews, bounties, and adventures
- Using data science to explore and uncover patterns in this universe.



# Background Information 
One piece is a long time running (1999) anime/ manga about pirates looking for the One Piece,
the greatest treasure in the world.

The show follows the Strawhat Crew, with the main character being Monkey D. Luffy, along with his friends including
Zoro, Sanji, Nami, Brook, Robin, Jinbe, & Chopper.

One Piece is essentially about pirate crews exploring different regions and competing for power, fame, and treasure. 
(And for Luffy to make friends along the way)

# What is a "Crew"?
A crew is a team of pirates 
Each crew has: 
- Captain (Leader of the group) 
- Members with roles (Navigator, Doctor, etc.)


# What is a "Bounty"?
- A bounty is a price put on the head of certain
individuals, typically pirates. 
- the higher the bounty, the more dangerous
the world government identifies you as


# What is a "Devil Fruit"?
- A devil fruit is a magical fruit in the One Piece
universe. 
- grant specific powers to the individual
- each fruit has a different power
- The catch?

# Tidying Data

- Lots of cleaning up 
- Some things were in french...
- nested dataframes
- and yea



```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Data cleaning

library(jsonlite)
library(tidyverse)

## Character(s) Data 
url1 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/characters/en"
characters <- fromJSON(url1)
characters_df <- as.data.frame(characters)


## Locations Data
url2 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/locates/en"
locations <- fromJSON(url2)
locations_df <- as.data.frame(locations)


## Episodes Data 
url3 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/episodes/en"
episodes <- fromJSON(url3)
episodes_df <- as.data.frame(episodes)

# crews data
url4 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/crews/en"
crews <- fromJSON(url4)
crews_df <- as.data.frame(crews)

# devil fruit data
url5 <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/fruits/en"
fruits <- fromJSON(url5)
fruits_df <- as.data.frame(fruits)
```




```{r include=FALSE}
# data cleaning

# converted some data types from string to numeric
characters_df <- characters_df %>%
  mutate(bounty = as.numeric(str_remove_all(bounty, "\\.")), 
         age = as.numeric(str_remove_all(age, "ans")),
         size = as.numeric(str_remove_all(size, "cm")))

# removed columns from both dataframes
# re-rerun every new session (can only be ran once since it gives error a second time)
crews_df <- crews_df %>% 
  select(-description)

crews_df <- crews_df %>% 
  select(-roman_name)

characters_df
crews_df

```

> Join characters with crew df

```{r include=FALSE}
# data cleaning

# crew df inside characters df
crew_full_df <- as.data.frame(characters_df$crew)

# converting data types
crew_full_df <- crew_full_df %>%
  mutate(
    total_prime = as.numeric(str_remove_all(total_prime, "\\.")),
    number = as.numeric(str_remove_all(number, "\\."))
  ) 

# removed the crew & joined the actual crew df
characters_with_crews <- bind_cols(
  characters_df %>% select(-crew),
  crew_full_df %>% rename(
    crew_id = id,
    crew_name = name,
    crew_status = status,
    crew_number = number,
    crew_total_prime = total_prime)) %>% 
    select(-description, -roman_name)

# translating some crew names from french to english
characters_with_crews <- characters_with_crews %>%
  mutate(
    crew_name = case_when(
      crew_name == "The Chapeau de Paille crew" ~ "Straw Hat Pirates",
      crew_name == "Armarda du Chapeau de Paille" ~ "Straw Hat Grand Fleet",
      crew_name == "Baggy's Delivery" ~ "Buggy's Delivery",
      crew_name == "Le Roux crew" ~ "Red Hair Pirates",
      TRUE ~ crew_name
    )
  )

# ignore this just use full_character_df
```

Devil Fruit joined with the rest of the DF

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# data merging + cleaning

# fruits df column
dev_fruit_df <- as.data.frame(characters_with_crews$fruit) %>%
  select(-roman_name, -filename, -technicalFile)   # removing unwanted columns

# merging table
full_character_df <- bind_cols(
  characters_with_crews %>% select(-fruit),
  dev_fruit_df %>%
    rename(devil_fruit = name) %>%
    select(-id)
)

full_character_df
```

# Bounty

In One Piece, the more chaos you cause, the higher the bounty on your head is. The bounties reflect how dangerous each pirate is. 


- Average bounty by age
```{r}
full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(bounty), !is.na(age)) %>%
  group_by(age) %>%
  summarise(mean_bounty = mean(bounty)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = mean_bounty)) +
  geom_line(color = "deeppink", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Bounty by Age",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Mean Bounty"
  ) +
  theme_get()
```


What percentage of characters have a bounty?

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# computation
library(gt)

full_character_df %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    with_bounty = sum(!is.na(bounty)),
    without_bounty = sum(is.na(bounty)),
    pct_with_bounty = mean(!is.na(bounty)) * 100
  ) %>%
  mutate(pct_with_bounty = paste0(round(pct_with_bounty, 1), "%")) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Bounty Summary"
  )

# visualization
full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_bounty = if_else(is.na(bounty), "No", "Yes")) %>%
  count(has_bounty) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = has_bounty, y = n, fill = has_bounty)) +
  geom_col(binwidth = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Yes" = "darkcyan",   # characters WITH bounty
      "No"  = "deeppink"      # characters WITHOUT bounty
    )
  ) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.3)+
  labs(
    title = "How Many Characters Have a Bounty?",
    x = "Has Bounty",
    y = "Count"
  )
```

# What is the average bounty between all pirates?
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
full_character_df %>%
  summarise(
    avg_bounty = mean(bounty, na.rm = T)
  )
```

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# had to count big moms crew since it didnt have it in crew_number
big_mom_count <- full_character_df %>%
  filter(crew_name == "Big Mom's crew") %>%
  summarise(n_members = n_distinct(name))

big_mom_count

full_character_df <- full_character_df %>%
  mutate(
    crew_number = ifelse(
      crew_name == "Big Mom's crew" & is.na(crew_number),
      big_mom_count$n_members,
      crew_number
    )
  )

```


Which 5 crew's has the highest bounty in total?
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# table
crews_high_bounty <- full_character_df %>%
  select(crew_name, crew_total_prime) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  slice_max(order_by = crew_total_prime, n = 5)
crews_high_bounty

#plot
crews_high_bounty %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = crew_total_prime / 1e6,
             y = reorder(crew_name, crew_total_prime))) +
  geom_point(size = 5, color = "darkcyan") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(crew_total_prime / 1e6, 1)),
            hjust = -0.3, size = 4, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 Crews by Total Bounty",
    x = "Total Bounty (Millions of Berries)",
    y = "Crew"
  ) +
  xlim(0, max(crews_high_bounty$crew_total_prime / 1e6) * 1.15)


# crew members plot
crews_high_bounty %>%
  left_join(
    full_character_df %>% 
      select(crew_name, crew_number) %>% 
      distinct(),
    by = "crew_name"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(
    x = crew_total_prime / 1e9,
    size = crew_number,
    y = crew_number,
    color = crew_name,
    label = crew_name 
  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(size = 3, vjust = -1) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 Crews: Total Bounty vs Crew Size",
    x = "Total Bounty (Billions of Berries)",
    y = "Crew Size",
    color = "Crew"         
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(5, 15))
```


What is the distribution of bounties? (high or low)
```{r}

```


# Do the captains have a higher bounty than the fighters?

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
library(kableExtra)
characters_with_crews %>%
  filter(job %in% c("Captain", "Fighter")) %>%
  group_by(job) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_bounty = mean(bounty, na.rm = TRUE),
    #maybe we can add a median here too
    count = n()
  ) %>%
  kbl() %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = F, position = "left")

```
thinking median can be added because the numbers can be extreme with us only having a mean 
would make the average look way bigger than normal. 




# Devil Fruits
Devil Fruits are considered rare and powerful items scattered throughout the world of One Piece, but it's unclear how they are distributed amoung characters and how they influence factors like bounties. 

# Why Study Devil Fruits? 
- Known to be rare and powerful 
- May influence strength and reputation 
- We wanted to see how they appear in the data

# Characters With vs Without Devil Fruits
- Shows how many characters have Devil Fruits 
- Majority of characters do not have one

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}

full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_fruit = ifelse(is.na(devil_fruit), "No Fruit", "Has Fruit")) %>%
  count(has_fruit) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = has_fruit)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Has Fruit" = "darkorchid", "No Fruit" = "slateblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Characters with vs Without Devil Fruits",
    fill = "Category"
  )+ 
  theme_minimal()
```

## Bar Graph (Correct Graph)
- Compared to the circle graph, this one is better

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_fruit = ifelse(is.na(devil_fruit), "No Fruit", "Has Fruit")) %>%
  count(has_fruit) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = has_fruit, y = n, fill = has_fruit)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Has Fruit" = "darkorchid", "No Fruit" = "slateblue")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Characters with vs Without Devil Fruits",
    x = "Category",
    y = "Number of Characters",
    fill = "Category"
  ) 
```

# Most Common Devil Fruit Types
- Compares different types of Devil Fruits 
- Some types appear more frequently than others

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
fruit_counts <- full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(type)) %>%
  count(type, sort = TRUE)

fruit_counts

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(fruit_counts, 
       aes(x = type, 
           y = n, 
           fill = type)) + geom_col() + geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.3, color = "navy") +
  labs(
  x = "Devil Fruit Type", 
  y = "Total",
  color = "Fruit Type", 
  title = "Most Common Devil Fruit Types"
) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```


# Crews with Multiple Devil Fruit Users 
- Shows crews with more than one user 
- Highlights where Devil Fruits are more concentrated 
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}

crew_fruit_counts <- full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(devil_fruit), !is.na(crew_name)) %>%   # ← this removes NA
  count(crew_name, sort = TRUE) %>%
  filter(n > 1)

crew_fruit_counts

ggplot(crew_fruit_counts, aes(x = reorder(crew_name, n), y = n, fill = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.2) +
  coord_flip()+
  labs(
    title = "Number of Devil Fruit Users per Crew",
    x = "Crew",
    y = "Number of Pirates"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")+ 
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "orange") 
```


# Do Devil Fruits Affect Bounty?
- Devil Fruits provide strong abilites
- Bounties reflect danger and influence 
- Do these two relate?

# Do Devil Fruit users have higher bounties?
- Compares bounty distribution between groups 
- Median bounty is higher for devil fruit users 
- Some non-users still have high bounties (outliers)
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

bounty_df <- full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_fruit = ifelse(is.na(devil_fruit), "No Fruit", "Has Fruit")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(bounty))


ggplot(bounty_df, aes(x = has_fruit, y = bounty, fill = has_fruit)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Has Fruit" = "darkorchid", "No Fruit" = "plum2")) +
  labs(
    title = "Bounty Distribution (Log Scale): Devil Fruit Users vs Non-Users",
    x = "Category",
    y = "Bounty (log scale)"
  ) 
```


# Average bounty of Devil Fruit users vs non-users?
- Comparing average bounty between the two groups 
- devil fruit users have a higher bounty average
-further supports data seen in the box plot
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_fruit = ifelse(is.na(devil_fruit), "No Fruit", "Has Fruit")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(bounty)) %>%
  group_by(has_fruit) %>%
  summarise(avg_bounty = mean(bounty, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = has_fruit, y = avg_bounty, fill = has_fruit)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_bounty, 0)), vjust = -0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Has Fruit" = "darkorchid", "No Fruit" = "slateblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Bounty: Devil Fruit Users vs Non-Users",
    x = "Category",
    y = "Average Bounty"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


# Highest bounty characters without Devil Fruits?
- Some non-Devil Fruit users still have high bounties 
- Strength and influence are not limited to Devil Fruit powers 

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
full_character_df %>%
  filter(is.na(devil_fruit), !is.na(bounty)) %>%
  arrange(desc(bounty)) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(name, bounty), y = bounty, fill = name)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(bounty/1e6), "M")), hjust = -0.2) + 
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Gol D. Roger" = "darkred",
    "Shanks" = "firebrick",
    "Roronoa Zoro" = "green4",
    "Jinbe" = "skyblue2",
    "Sanji" = "gold"
  ))
  labs(
    title = "Highest Bounty Characters Without Devil Fruits",
    x = "Character",
    y = "Bounty"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

# Islands by Region
- Compares islands across regions 
- Shows which seas/regions have the most islands in the dataset

## East Blue vs North Blue
- Focuses on two secific regions 
- Makes the comparison easier to see

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
locations_df

blue_counts <- locations_df %>%
  filter(sea_name %in% c("East Blue", "North Blue")) %>%
  count(sea_name)


ggplot(blue_counts, aes(x = sea_name, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Islands: East Blue vs North Blue",
    x = "Sea",
    y = "Number of Islands"
  )
```

# Island  Distribution by Region
- Compares number of islands across regions 
Some regions appear more frequently in the dataset
- Aligns with anime/manga
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
locations_df %>%
  filter(sea_name != "", !is.na(sea_name)) %>%   # remove empty + real NA
  count(sea_name) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sea_name, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkcyan") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n),
            vjust = -0.3, 
            size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "How Many Islands Are in Each Region?",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Number of Islands"
  ) 
```

# Characters

How many members are in the Strawhats crew?
```{r}
characters_with_crews %>%
  filter(crew_name == "Straw Hat Pirates") %>% 
  summarise(total_members = n())

```

How many members are in the Big Mom crew? (comparing to other crews)
```{r}
crew_counts <- full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(crew_name)) %>%
  count(crew_name, sort = TRUE) %>%
  filter(n > 6)

ggplot(crew_counts, aes(x = reorder(crew_name, n), y = n,
                        fill = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "plum1", high = "magenta3") +
  labs(
    title = "Crew Size Comparison (Crews with More Than 6 Members)",
    x = "Crew",
    y = "Number of Members"
  )+
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

How many characters are captains of their crew? 
```{r}

characters_with_crews %>%
  filter(job == "Captain") %>%
  summarise(total_captains = n()) 

```

<<<<<<< HEAD
What occupations appear most often in pirate crews?
=======
# How many captains are there compared to navigators? 
>>>>>>> 60670da7c70d27407799a13bc17f1f60942dbbf0
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

job_counts <- characters_with_crews %>%
  filter(!is.na(job), job != "") %>%
  count(job, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 10)

ggplot(job_counts, aes(x = reorder(job, n), y = n, fill = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "plum1", high = "magenta3") +
  labs(
    title = "Most Common Occupations in Pirate Crews",
    x = "Occupation",
    y = "Number of Characters"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")



#characters_with_crews %>%
 # filter(job %in% c("Captain", "Navigator")) %>%
  #distinct(name, job) %>%
  #count(job, sort = T)

#lets change this question up a bit, maybe include doctors, or just general crew members 
```

# Distribution of character sizes
What is the distribution of height among the characters? 

```{r}
# boxplot
full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(size)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = size)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1500)) +     # <-- sets max x to 2000 cm
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Character Heights",
    x = "Height (cm)",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# density
full_character_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(size)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = size)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), binwidth = 5,
                 fill = "gray80", color = "gray40") +
  geom_density(color = "blue", size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Character Size Distribution with Density Overlay",
    x = "Size (cm)",
    y = "Density"
  )

# fruit user vs non fruit users
full_character_df %>%
  mutate(has_fruit = !is.na(devil_fruit)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = size, fill = has_fruit)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(title = "Size Distribution: Fruit Users vs Non‑Users")
```


# Episodes
```{r}
episodes_df
```
How many episodes were released per decade?
```{r}

```

What year had the most episode release?
```{r}

```

What month has the most episode releases?
```{r}

```

What is the average amount of chapters per episode?
```{r}

```
